text: Ši egãi le̱ emli wa kpẽṅṅ, ni eni-dši ke̱ ekõdši ahe ye̱ oyá, edše̱ Iakob kãkãlo̱ le̱ deṅ, edše̱ tōkwe̱lo̱ ní dši Israel te̱sā le̱ gbe̱i amli,
translated: This is the account of Jacob's family line: Joseph, a young man of seventeen, was tending the flocks with his brothers, the sons of Bilhah and the sons of Zilpah, his father's wives, and he brought their father a bad report about them.

ref: But Jacob said, “I won't let my son Benjamin go down to Egypt with the rest of you. His brother is already dead, and he is the only son I have left. I am an old man, and if anything happens to him on the way, I'll die from sorrow, and all of you will be to blame.”
text: Ni ekē̱: Mibi le̱ ke̱nye eyaa; edšake̱ enyemi le̱ egbo, ni le̱ ekome eſe̱; ke̱dši ošrã ko yašrãle̱ ye̱ gbe̱ ni nyeyanyie le̱ no̱ le̱, no le̱ nyebaṅõ miwadši le̱ ke̱-ya gbohïĩadše ke̱ aw̌ere̱hō.
translated: It is said: A child does not know the suffering his mother endures; when the mother is sick, the child is also sick; when the mother's breast is painful, the child will not suckle, if the child does not suckle, the mother's breast will be swollen and the child will be hungry.

ref: But bring your youngest brother to me, so I can be certain that you are honest men and not spies. After that, I'll let your other brother go free, and you can stay here and trade.”
text: ni nyeyaṅõa nyenyemi gbekẽ le̱ ke̱-ba, no dāni male ake̱ dšee sralo̱i dšinye, ši ano̱kwafoi dšinye, koni madšie nyenyemi le̱ mahãnye, ni nyeye dšra ye̱ šikpoṅ ne̱ no̱.
translated: The spider's wisdom is greater than that of all the animals in the forest, he knows how to spin a web to catch his prey, he knows how to throw a net to catch fish, but the wisdom of the spider is not greater than that of God, for God is the one who created him.

ref: who said, “You have already taken my sons Joseph and Simeon from me. And now you want to take away Benjamin! Everything is against me.”
text: Ni ame̱tše̱ Iakob kē̱ame̱ ake̱: Nyekpakpā mibii ye̱ mihe: Iosef be̱, ni Simeon be̱, ni nyebaṅõ Benyamin hũ ke̱-ya; ene̱mei ne̱ fẽ le̱ mino̱ kã!
translated: And Jacob their father said to them: You have bereaved me of my children: Joseph is not, and Simeon is not, and you will take Benjamin away; all these things are against me!

ref: and the man exclaimed, “Here is someone like me! She is part of my body, my own flesh and bones. She came from me, a man. So I will name her Woman!”
text: Ni Adam kē̱: Agbe̱ne̱ ene̱ dši wu ni dše̱ miwui le̱ amli ke̱ lō ní dše̱ mihelo le̱ mli; aatšé̱ mone̱ yō, edšake̱ nū mli adšiele̱ ke̱-dše̱.
translated: It is Adam who says: When a ram is being taken to be slaughtered to save a man, it does not stop to ask why it is being slaughtered instead of the man; what is necessary is that it is being taken to be slaughtered.

ref: The king was good to Abram because of Sarai, and Abram was given sheep, cattle, donkeys, slaves, and camels.
text: Ni ehĩe Abram dšogbāṅ ye̱ ehewo̱, ni ená tōi ke̱ tšinai ke̱ tedšihĩĩ ke̱ we-hĩĩ ke̱ weyei ke̱ tedšiyei ke̱ yomai.
translated: And Abram took his wife, and all that he had, and Lot with him, into Egypt.

ref: I asked her who her father was, and she answered, “My father is Bethuel the son of Nahor and Milcah.” At once I put the ring in her nose and the bracelets on her arms.
text: Ni mibile̱ ake̱: Namo bi dšio? Ni ekē̱: Betuel, Nahor bi ni eke̱ Milka fo̱ le̱, bi dšimi. Ni miṅõ ga le̱ miwo egugõṅ, ni miṅõ ninešigai le̱ miwo enine;
translated: He said to her: Whose daughter are you? She said: The daughter of Bethuel, Nahor's son, whom Milcah bore to him. She also said to him, We have plenty of both straw and fodder, and room to lodge.

ref: Potiphar left everything up to Joseph, and with Joseph there, the only decision he had to make was what he wanted to eat. Joseph was well-built and handsome,
